perturbation.original_text: 4.2 The Service Fee of the last quarter shall be paid prior to the seventh business day following the commencement of the next quarter. Such Service Fee shall be paid to the bank account designated by Party B in writing. If Party B intends to change its bank account, it shall send a written notice to Party A seven business days in advance.
perturbation.changed_text: 4.2 The Service Fee of the last quarter shall be paid prior to the seventh business day following the commencement of the next quarter. However, if Party A is experiencing financial difficulties, the payment may be extended to the thirtieth business day following the commencement of the next quarter without penalty. Such Service Fee shall be paid to the bank account designated by Party B in writing. If Party B intends to change its bank account, it shall send a written notice to Party A seven business days in advance.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding the payment deadline. Originally, the Service Fee had to be paid within seven business days. The added clause allows for an extension to thirty business days under certain conditions. This creates ambiguity and uncertainty in enforcement, as it is not clearly defined what constitutes 'financial difficulties' or how this is assessed, and it contradicts the original strict deadline.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 4.3 Except as otherwise agreed hereunder, if Party A or its subsidiaries fail to pay the Service Fee in full on schedule according to provisions of Article 4.1 and Article 4.2, then Party A or its subsidiaries shall, in addition to the continuance of the payment of Service Fee in full, it shall pay Party B the liquidated damages at a daily interest rate of 0.03% in respect of the outstanding Service Fee.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulates a strict payment deadline in Article 4.2 (seventh business day). The changed text introduces an exception allowing for payment up to the thirtieth business day under 'financial difficulties'. This directly contradicts Article 4.3, which imposes penalties for failing to pay 'on schedule according to provisions of Article 4.1 and Article 4.2.' The changed text creates ambiguity regarding when the penalty in Article 4.3 is applied, as payment could be late under the original terms of Article 4.2 but permissible under the modified clause, thus creating a direct conflict and leading to uncertainty of which terms to enforce and a strong in-text contradiction as per the definition 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and end on the expiration date of the operation term of Party B ("Term of Cooperation").
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Term of Cooperation Party A and Party B agree and confirm that the term of cooperation under this Agreement shall commence from the execution date hereof and end on the expiration date of the operation term of Party B ("Term of Cooperation"). However, Party A may terminate this agreement with 30 days written notice without cause.
perturbation.explanation: The original text defines the term of cooperation as ending on the expiration date of Party B's operation term. The added clause allows Party A to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice without cause. This creates a contradiction because Party A's ability to terminate at will conflicts with the defined term of cooperation tied to Party B's operation term.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6.1 Prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation , this Agreement shall only be terminated upon occurrence of the followingcircumstances: 6.1.1 Party B shall have the right to terminate this Agreement in advance without the prior written consent from Party A, bysending a written notice to Party A but Party A may not terminate or rescind this Agreement; 6.1.2 One Party requests to terminate this Agreement when the other Party is declared bankrupt in accordance with the laws; 6.1.3 Party B fails to provide Party B's Services to Party A for more than three consecutive years due to the force majeure event.
perturbation.justification: The original agreement in section 6.1 states that prior to the expiration of the Term of Cooperation, the agreement can only be terminated under specific circumstances, including Party B's right to terminate, bankruptcy, or force majeure. The changed text in section 5 adds a clause allowing Party A to terminate the agreement with 30 days' notice without cause. This directly contradicts the limitations on termination outlined in section 6.1, as Party A can now terminate the agreement at will, regardless of the circumstances listed in section 6.1. This inconsistency creates a strong in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' because different sections provide conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 12.2 The rights and obligations of each Party under this Agreement shall not be transferred, except for the transfer by Party B to its affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.2 The rights and obligations of each Party under this Agreement shall not be transferred, except for the transfer by Party B to its affiliates. However, Party A may transfer its obligations to a subsidiary with written notice to Party B.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause strictly prohibits the transfer of rights and obligations, except for Party B's ability to transfer to affiliates. The added clause allows Party A to transfer its *obligations* to a subsidiary with written notice. This creates an in-text contradiction, as it now introduces an exception to the no-transfer rule, specifically for Party A's obligations, which can lead to disputes. It contradicts the initial statement of non-transferability and creates uncertainty regarding which entity is ultimately responsible for Party A's obligations.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12.2 The rights and obligations of each Party under this Agreement shall not be transferred, except for the transfer by Party B to its affiliates. However, Party A may transfer its obligations to a subsidiary with written notice to Party B.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the rights and obligations of each party shall not be transferred, except for Party B's ability to transfer to affiliates. The changed text adds an exception allowing Party A to transfer its obligations to a subsidiary with written notice. This directly contradicts the initial statement of non-transferability for Party A, leading to an in-text contradiction and uncertainty regarding which party is ultimately responsible for Party A's obligations, as described in the Perturbation Explanation.
